assertions.title: Returning (single) secondary actions use chip-style with "or" prefix
assertions.rationale: On /demo/welcome-returning-single, the secondary actions are shown in an 'or' row as compact chips (New Keyset, Import Device Profile, Onboard), visually distinct from primary buttons.

steps.expected: Secondary actions render as chip-style controls prefixed by literal 'or'
steps.observed: Observed 'or' label and chip-style New Keyset / Import Device Profile / Onboard controls

assertions.title: Returning (single) Unlock opens unlock modal
assertions.rationale: Clicking Unlock opened modal overlay with header 'Unlock Profile' and identity subtitle 'My Signing Key · 2/3 · #0'.

steps.expected: Unlock modal opens with header and required identity subtitle
steps.observed: Modal opened with 'Unlock Profile' and 'My Signing Key · 2/3 · #0'

evidence.network: UI modal interaction only (no failing requests observed)

assertions.title: Returning (single) New Keyset chip navigates to /create
assertions.rationale: From /demo/welcome-returning-single, clicking New Keyset navigated to /create.

evidence.network: Navigation to /create observed via URL transition

assertions.title: Returning (single) Import Device Profile chip navigates to /import
assertions.rationale: From /demo/welcome-returning-single, clicking Import Device Profile navigated to /import.

evidence.network: Navigation to /import observed via URL transition
Navigation to /onboard observed via URL transition

assertions.title: Returning (multi) renders core content
assertions.rationale: On /demo/welcome-returning-multi, observed Welcome back heading, stacked profile rows for My Signing Key / Work Key / Backup Key with per-row Unlock+Rotate, and chip-style 'or' row.

steps.expected: Core multi-profile content and per-row controls are visible
steps.observed: All required profile rows, subtitles, controls, and chip row were visible

assertions.title: Returning (multi) uses per-profile rows (variant distinction)
assertions.rationale: The returning-multi screen shows multiple full-width stacked profile cards, each with its own Unlock and Rotate controls, distinct from first-time and many variants.

steps.expected: Stacked per-profile rows with per-row actions (variant distinction)
steps.observed: Observed three stacked profile cards with independent Unlock/Rotate pairs and no many-variant overflow/count markers

summary: Tested 7 assigned assertions (VAL-WEL-008..014): 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
